fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Hampshire Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to New Hampshire - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity new hampshire, how to transfer dietitian license to new hampshire, dietitian compact new hampshire, new hampshire dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Board of Licensed Dietitians
board.parent_dept: New Hampshire Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC)
board.mailing_address:
Office of Professional Licensure and Certification
7 Eagle Square
Concord, NH 03301

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice dietetics in New Hampshire, overseen by the OPLC Board of Licensed Dietitians.

steps.title: Meet Eligibility Requirements
steps.description: Ensure you possess a bachelor's degree or higher in dietetics/nutrition from a regionally accredited institution and have completed a planned, supervised practice of at least 900 hours, approved by the Accreditation Council for Education in Nutrition and Dietetics (ACEND) or the Board.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Exam
steps.description: Successfully pass the board-approved examination for dietitians, typically administered by the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Create an OPLC Account
steps.description: Visit the NH Online Licensing Portal (https://www.oplc.nh.gov/oplc-online-licensing-portal) and create a new account if you don't already have one.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the NH Online Licensing Portal, select the 'Dietitian' license type, and complete the online application form for initial licensure or endorsement.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload or arrange for submission of all required documentation, including official transcripts, verification of supervised practice, and official CDR exam scores, to the OPLC.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Pay the required $100 application fee through the NH Online Licensing Portal. An additional initial licensure fee will be due upon approval.

documents.detail: A letter verifying RD status sent directly from the Commission on Dietetic Registration to the Board.
Required if RD card was obtained on or after 1/1/2001. Must be sent from the degree-granting institution.
Required if RD card was obtained on or after 1/1/2001. Sent directly from the internship supervisor.

provisional_text: New Hampshire regulations generally require the completion of the CDR examination prior to issuing a license. There is no standard provision for a temporary dietitian license.
reciprocity_text: New Hampshire may grant a license to any applicant who presents proof of current licensure, registration, or certification as a dietitian in a state or territory whose requirements are substantially equivalent to New Hampshire's, alongside the standard application and fee.

faqs.question: How much does a New Hampshire dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $110.

faqs.question: When does the New Hampshire license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: New Hampshire dietitian licenses must be renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $110. A $150 reinstatement fee applies to late submissions.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Licensees must complete 20 hours of approved continuing education within the preceding two years to qualify for license renewal.

faqs.question: Does New Hampshire have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: New Hampshire has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to New Hampshire?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Hampshire while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: New Hampshire may grant a license to any applicant who presents proof of current licensure, registration, or certification as a dietitian in a state or territory whose requirements are substantially equivalent to New Hampshire's, alongside the standard application and fee.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in New Hampshire
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian license in New Hampshire, you must use the NH Online Licensing Portal, managed by the Office of Professional Licensure and Certification (OPLC).

variants.label: New Applicant

variants.label: Reciprocity

official_links.registration_steps: Visit the NH Online Licensing Portal, click 'Register' to create a user ID and password, then log in to start your application.
